perturbation.original_text: In accordance with 41 C.F.R.ss.60-1.4(a), the parties incorporate herein by this reference the regulations and contract clauses required by that section, including but not limited to, Contractor's agreement that it will not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, religion, sex, or national origin. The Contractor will take affirmative action to ensure that applicants are employed, and that employees are treated during employment, without regard to their race, color, religion, sex, or national origin.
perturbation.changed_text: The Contractor agrees that it will not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment. The Contractor will take action to ensure that employees are treated during employment.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific protected classes (race, color, religion, sex, or national origin) and weakening the 'affirmative action' commitment, the contract potentially violates federal anti-discrimination laws. Also the reference to 41 C.F.R.ss.60-1.4(a) has been removed.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964
perturbation.law_explanation: Title VII prohibits discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex, or national origin. The modified text removes these protected categories, creating a legal contradiction as the contractor's commitment is now less comprehensive than required by law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 | U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission Skip to main content Official websites use .gov A .gov website belongs to an official government organization in the United States. Secure .gov websites use HTTPS A lock ( Lock A locked padlock ) or https:// means you’ve safely connected to the .gov website. Share sensitive information only on official, secure websites. Home statutes Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 EDITOR'S NOTE: The following is the text of Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 (Title VII) (Pub. L. 88-352), as amended. Title VII is codified at 42 U.S.C. 2000e and in subsequent sections. Title VII amendments include those introduced by the Civil Rights Act of 1991 (CRA) and the Lilly Ledbetter Fair Pay Act of 2009 . Editor’s notes appear in italics and include cross-references to the codified version of Title VII. Title VII prohibits employment discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex and national origin. An Act To enforce the constitutional right to vote, to confer jurisdiction upon the district courts of the United States to provide injunctive relief against discrimination in public accommodations, to authorize the attorney General to institute suits to protect constitutional rights in public facilities and public education, to extend the Commission on Civil Rights, to prevent discrimination in federally assisted programs, to establish a Commission on Equal Employment Opportunity, and for other purposes. Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the United States of America in Congress assembled, That this Act may be cited as the "Civil Rights Act of 1964". * * * DEFINITIONS SEC. 2000e. [Section 701] For the purposes of this subchapter- (a) The term "person" includes one or more individuals, governments, governmental agencies, political subdivisions, labor unions, partnerships, associations, corporations, legal representatives, mutual companies, joint-­stock companies, trusts, unincorporated organizations, trustees, trustees in cases under Title 11 [originally, bankruptcy ], or receivers. (b) The term "employer" means a person engaged in an industry affecting commerce who has fifteen or more employees for each working day in each of twenty or more calendar weeks in the current or preceding calendar year, and any agent of such a person, but such term does not include (1) the United States, a corporation wholly owned by the Government of the United States, an Indian tribe, or any department or agency of the District of Columbia subject by statute to procedures of the competitive s

perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific protected classes (race, color, religion, sex, or national origin) outlined in the original text and Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964. Scraped Law Snippet 1 explicitly states, 'Title VII prohibits employment discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex and national origin.' By omitting these categories, the contractor's commitment is weakened and less comprehensive than required by law. The omission of 'affirmative action' further weakens the commitment, moving away from the original intent to actively ensure equal opportunity.

perturbation.original_text: In accordance with 41 C.F.R.ss.60-1.8, Contractor agrees that it does not and will not maintain or provide for its employees any facilities segregated on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, or national origin at any of its establishments, and that it does not and will not permit its employees to perform their services at any location, under its control, where such segregated facilities are maintained.
perturbation.changed_text: Contractor agrees that it will not maintain or provide for its employees any facilities segregated at any of its establishments, and that it will not permit its employees to perform their services at any location, under its control, where such segregated facilities are maintained.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the protected classes ('race, color, religion, sex, or national origin'), the contract weakens its commitment to non-segregation, potentially violating federal regulations and anti-discrimination laws. The reference to 41 C.F.R.ss.60-1.8 has been removed.
perturbation.law_explanation: 41 C.F.R. § 60-1.8 specifically prohibits segregated facilities based on race, color, religion, sex, or national origin. The modified text removes these categories, thereby falling short of the regulatory requirement and creating a legal contradiction.

This process will be necessary for each IP address you wish to access the site from, requests are valid for approximately one quarter (three months) after which the process may need to be repeated. If you want to request a wider IP range, first request access for your current IP, and then use the "Site Feedback" button found in the lower left-hand side to make the request.
perturbation.justification: The original contract explicitly prohibits segregation based on 'race, color, religion, sex, or national origin' as per 41 C.F.R.ss.60-1.8. The modified text removes these protected classes. This omission creates a legal contradiction because it weakens the non-segregation commitment, deviating from the specific requirements outlined in the regulation. Although the scraped law snippets are not helpful for this evaluation, the law explanation provides that 41 C.F.R. § 60-1.8 requires these specific categories, and their removal contradicts this requirement.

perturbation.original_text: AFFIRMATIVE ACTION FOR HANDICAPPED PERSONS AND DISABLED VETERANS, VETERANS OF THE VIETNAM ERA. In accordance with 41 C.F.R.ss.60-250.20 and 41 C.F.R.ss.60-741.20, the parties incorporate herein by this reference the regulations and contract clauses required by those provisions to be made a part of government contracts and subcontracts.
perturbation.changed_text: AFFIRMATIVE ACTION. The parties incorporate herein by this reference the regulations and contract clauses required to be made a part of government contracts and subcontracts.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the specific reference to 'HANDICAPPED PERSONS AND DISABLED VETERANS, VETERANS OF THE VIETNAM ERA' and removing the CFR references, the contract weakens the affirmative action requirements for these protected groups, creating a contradiction with regulations requiring specific affirmative action plans for them.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Vietnam Era Veterans' Readjustment Assistance Act of 1974 (VEVRAA)
perturbation.law_explanation: VEVRAA requires affirmative action for disabled veterans. The modified text omits this specific requirement, leading to non-compliance.

perturbation.justification: The original contract language specifically mentions 'HANDICAPPED PERSONS AND DISABLED VETERANS, VETERANS OF THE VIETNAM ERA' and references 41 C.F.R.ss.60-250.20 and 41 C.F.R.ss.60-741.20, which pertain to affirmative action requirements for these groups. The changed text removes this specific mention, weakening the affirmative action requirements. The Law Explanation states that VEVRAA requires affirmative action for disabled veterans and the modified text omits this, leading to non-compliance. This omission contradicts the legal obligations for affirmative action towards these protected groups, which the original text explicitly addressed. The Scraped Law Snippets are not helpful in this case.

perturbation.original_text: UTILIZATION OF SMALL, SMALL DISADVANTAGED AND WOMEN-OWNED SMALL BUSINESS CONCERNS As prescribed in 48 C.F.R., Ch. 1, 19.708(a): (a) It is the policy of the United states that small business concerns, small business concerns owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals and small business concerns owned and controlled by women shall have the maximum practicable opportunity to participate in performing contracts let by any Federal agency, including contracts and sub-contracts for systems, assemblies, components, and related services for major systems. It is further the policy of the United States that its prime contractors establish procedures to ensure the timely payment amounts due pursuant to the terms of the subcontracts with small business concerns, small business concerns owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals and small business concerns owned and controlled by women.
perturbation.changed_text: UTILIZATION OF SMALL BUSINESS CONCERNS: (a) It is the policy of the United states that small business concerns shall have the opportunity to participate in performing contracts let by any Federal agency. It is further the policy of the United States that its prime contractors establish procedures to ensure the timely payment amounts due pursuant to the terms of the subcontracts with small business concerns.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the references to 'small business concerns owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals and small business concerns owned and controlled by women', the contract weakens its commitment to supporting these specific types of small businesses, potentially violating regulations aimed at promoting their participation in federal contracts. The reference to 48 C.F.R., Ch. 1, 19.708(a) has been removed.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Small Business Act emphasizes opportunities for disadvantaged businesses. Omitting mention of these businesses reduces compliance.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: This page is better viewed with a JavaScript enabled browser. Document not Found Document not found The document you were looking for does not exist. Click here to close this window, or click here to go back.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifically mentions 'small business concerns owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals and small business concerns owned and controlled by women', emphasizing their inclusion in federal contracts. The changed text omits these specific categories, only referring to 'small business concerns'. According to the perturbation explanation, this weakens the contract's commitment to supporting these specific types of small businesses, potentially violating regulations aimed at promoting their participation in federal contracts. The law explanation supports this by stating that the Small Business Act emphasizes opportunities for disadvantaged businesses and omitting these businesses reduces compliance. The omission falls under the definition of Omission Legal Contradiction, where essential information about legally mandated consumer protection is removed, making the contract non-compliant.
